blocks.title: A2UI × Astryx
blocks.subtitle: Meta's component catalogue against the A2UI atom library. Not a size race — a shape read: where each spends its mass tells you what it's for.

blocks.title: The finding
items: Astryx ships 92 components; A2UI ships 498 — 5.4× larger, so breadth was never the gap.
~66 of Astryx's 92 already have A2UI equivalents — the gap is ~26, and it's one band.
That band is stateful INPUT + app chrome, headed by Chat.
Astryx is an interaction kit; A2UI is a presentation kit. Different centres of gravity.

blocks.title: An interaction kit
blocks.text: Data Input (19) is their biggest bet, plus a first-class 7-piece Chat system — Astryx is a chassis for stateful, agent-facing surfaces.

blocks.caption: Astryx category → what A2UI is missing
rows: Field, Typeahead/Power Search, Tokenizer, Date/Time, Number, File, Calendar
Toolbar, More Menu, Toggle Button (+group), Icon Button, Button Group
App Shell, Aspect Ratio, Resize Handle
Side Nav, Mega Menu (+ featured card)
VisuallyHidden — opens a track you have none of

blocks.title: Consolidation discipline
blocks.text: Fold the variants into mode/flags and ~26 gaps collapse to ~18 atoms — same principle as the chat router.
The Chat fit map — all 498 atoms

blocks.title: How A2UI atoms render on Google Chat
The printer changes this
blocks.text: The 177 image-only atoms were the dead bucket on Chat — until the printer. Render to an image, deliver as an attachment, and cardsV2's ceiling stops mattering for alerts.
Astryx is an interaction kit; A2UI is a presentation kit. You don't need their 92 — you need one band (stateful input, headed by Chat), and everything else is already yours, 5× over.
